docs.location: Closed Guard/Bottom/Kneeling Opponent/K Guard to Inverted Heel Hook/
docs.text: Drill this if just starting You need to try to lift his hips off the mat so that you can reach under the leg. Try to attack the arm bar or hand fight and pull knees to the chest. Make sure to frame when he lifts his hips If he grabs your head and arm to crush you, make sure you pummel your head. If you can’t pummel or kick him away, try to off balance him over your hips Shrimp hips to get knee to the inside. You can open your guard and put your foot on the mat to hip out Right hook goes to inside or outside armpit Pull the knee on top of your chest while pushing them away. Make sure you keep the grip tight on his leg. If his leg is past the knee line you can extend your back to bring it back in. Or, you can start sitting up with your arm underneath his hook to force him down and catch the heel Ideally shoot the leg all the way through to the far leg If he rolls keep your foot inside on his stomach, not out in 50/50. Then transition to 90/10
docs.title: K Guard to Inverted Heel Hook

docs.text: Sit up on entries to get better grab of the leg as well as to be able to switch back to de la riva
docs.title: Concepts

docs.text: 2 on 1 grip Sit up to reach the far leg If they pull their leg away, switch to the other leg
docs.title: K Guard Entry

docs.text: Triangle De La Riva Entry ¶ 2 on 1 Threaten the triangle with opposite leg Enter De La Riva Ankle Grab De La Riva Entry ¶ Keep your knees tight Grab his ankle Swing your hips over the top
docs.title: Entries

docs.text: 2 on 1 Threaten the triangle with opposite leg Enter De La Riva
docs.title: Triangle De La Riva Entry

docs.text: Keep your knees tight Grab his ankle Swing your hips over the top
docs.title: Ankle Grab De La Riva Entry

docs.location: Open Guard/Bottom/De La Riva/Standing Opponent/K guard to inverted heel hook/
docs.text: Right foot is active keeping distance If he’s driving forward, block the knee with the hand. If he is pulling away, use the right hand to prevent him from running Get a deep mantis grip at the elbow By pushing with your hand, it helps to shrimp the hips and invert Shrimp hips out to free knee to the inside of shoulder and on to the ground. The foot is blocking the knee from turning into you Connect the hands together to re-inforce the foot Right leg shoots over and pull tight to catch the leg close to the knee if possible but able to catch the ankle if he flees Don’t worry about leg drag because it will put you into the right position If they defend with 2 on 1 on the free leg, off balance first
docs.title: K guard to inverted heel hook

docs.text: Make sure you sit up before reaching for far leg. This allows you to curl in and get a deep grip. It is easy for your opponent to just kick back their leg if you reach from your back
docs.title: Elevation Over Hips

docs.text: Reverse De La Riva Knee Cut Entry ¶ Frame to keep distance Make sure your foot goes to his armpit first even before your RDLR hook
docs.title: Entries

docs.text: Frame to keep distance Make sure your foot goes to his armpit first even before your RDLR hook
docs.title: Reverse De La Riva Knee Cut Entry

docs.text: Keep your foot in his ribs/arm pit so that he has to lean back to clear it. When he leans back and he becomes off balance, you can go for your attack. If his shin is horizontal, then often reach to the heel vs deep underhook If his shin is more vertical, then often go to elbow deep overhook
docs.title: Concepts

docs.location: Open Guard/Bottom/Reverse De La Riva/Standing Opponent/Inverted 50 50 Heel Hook/
docs.text: If his shin is horizontal, reach for the foot Spin to invert but only go half way Right leg comes out and left hand grabs the leg Shoot the right leg over into 50/50 Finish rotation for heel hook
docs.title: Inverted 50 50 Heel Hook
